title: Rei Letting Instrumentality Start
wikitext:
[]: Rei Letting Instrumentality Start             Quote Post by HiImAnAlien » Wed Jul 09, 2014 7:44 am After the 2nd viewing of EoE I got to the conclusion that Shinji didn't knew that his desperation will lead to the Instrumentality. Rei didn't really explains it to him, in fact she lets his desperation grow until his Ego-barrier is destroyed, which is what truly leads to the instrumentality -not his statements about letting everybody die or him deserving to die. Rei doesn't make any attempt to make him change his mind. So, the fault is on Rei. But why did she let it happen? I only see 3 options: 1. She just went with what she perceived was Shinji's desire because she just changed masters. I don't like this option because it means that Rei's turn on Gendo signifies nothing. No main character got fully what they needed, but they got at least a huge chunk of it, which in turn may be enough in the long scheme of things. Asuka got to show that she's truly the best pilot, Shinji made a crucial decision both for him and others that was both life-affirming and displayed a level of emotional maturity for the future... Rei, as I said, just changed masters. I guess becoming a planet size Goddess is as good as it gets, but this has nothing to do with her personality. 2. Her plan to let Instrumentality happen was a huge -albeit questionable- rescue plan. Wee see her next to Misato's dead body and later we see Misato in the pre-instrumentality. We also see Asuka there so the logical conclusion was that she also took her soul. Why we don't see this happen is another thing altogether. Anyway, we then later see her near the dead bodies of other NERV personnel. Instrumentality ends, Asuka comes back to life and we are told everyone can do the same. So, it may have been a rescue plan on her turn all along. I would prefer it to be that way, because it means Rei is a boss. Don't agree with her decision, the risk being way too big and resting only on her faith in Shinji, but it definitely makes her a better character. 3. She couldn't choose between letting humanity be saved or turned into LCL because she couldn't choose between two sides of her. One as Lilith -which wanted Instrumentality to her children, and the other being the Rei that grew between humans and become somewhat close to them. So letting Shinji choose for her was her only option. The reason I bring this possibility is because it's stated by Rei in ep. 25. Whether or not you accept the last 2 episodes as concurrent with EoE, I still think bits of it can be at least used as explanatory for EoE. In this case, Rei saying that "death" is her wish but a wish she doesn't want. I think this contradiction can only be attributed to a dark side of her soul she doesn't agree with it, but rules her from the inside. Lilith The reason she may have chosen Shinji is because she related to him at that point, in the case of Shinji depression being the darker side of his soul. So, what do you think? Which one do you accept? Do you think it was something else. Which one do you prefer?

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Wed Jul 09, 2014 8:00 am Fourth option: 4. She had no choice because she's a Seed of Life, and because she came into contact with another Seed of Life all life on the planet was reset until a third party (Shinji) could make a decision on how to proceed. But 1 is far more likely. I really wish it was 2 -- it would give her a hell of a lot of agency, which she desperately needs -- but the narrative suggests 1.

[]: Re: Rei :letting Instrumentality start             Quote Post by Sorrow » Wed Jul 09, 2014 8:14 am HiImAnAlien wrote: 1. She just went with what she perceived was Shinji's desire because she just changed masters. I don't like this option because it means that Rei's turn on Gendo signifies nothing. No main character got fully what they needed, but they got at least a huge chunk of it, which in turn may be enough in the long scheme of things. Asuka got to show that she's truly the best pilot, Shinji made a crucial decision both for him and others that was both life-affirming and displayed a level of emotional maturity for the future... Rei, as I said, just changed masters. I guess becoming a planet size Goddess is as good as it gets, but this has nothing to do with her personality.

[]: Re: Rei :letting Instrumentality start             Quote Post by HiImAnAlien » Wed Jul 09, 2014 12:32 pm Sorrow wrote: I think it shows that Rei isn't a doll or simply a puppet. The fact seems clear that she grew to care about Shinji more than Gendo, and she uses that in her choice to give the right of instrumentality to Shinji and not Gendo. She herself wonders why she has started to replace Gendo with Shinji in her affections; she becomes more human and gains a degree of freedom. That is what she receives by the end of the series. She resolved to make Shinji happy, even if no one else, as her last act in life.

[]: Re: Rei :letting Instrumentality start             Quote Post by Sorrow » Wed Jul 09, 2014 12:56 pm HiImAnAlien wrote: It's a false freedom tough, cause she gives it up as soon as she gains it.

[]:             Quote Post by AuraTwilight » Wed Jul 09, 2014 1:29 pm Maybe she didn't go with what Shinji wanted because she 'changed masters' but because she came to selflessly care about his well-being in a way very few ever have before. That she can choose her own master means she can choose what to obey and what to disobey. Willing servants have agency; that's essentially the fundamental concept of Loyalty, honestly.

[]: Re: Rei :letting Instrumentality start             Quote Post by HiImAnAlien » Wed Jul 09, 2014 1:46 pm Sorrow wrote: "giving it up" would imply she had the freedom to do so.

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Wed Jul 09, 2014 1:57 pm I've long thought it was a combination of options 2 (don't forget Eva-01/Yui & Adam/Kaworu) and 4, myself.

[]: Re: Rei :letting Instrumentality start             Quote Post by Sorrow » Wed Jul 09, 2014 2:00 pm HiImAnAlien wrote: So you think she was forced to let someone else make the decision?

[]:             Quote Post by AuraTwilight » Thu Jul 10, 2014 1:21 pm That means she's still a servant. And Shinji didn't deserved that level of caring anyway. She deserved more caring than NERV gave him but surely not that much.

[]: Re: Rei :letting Instrumentality start             Quote Post by Kendrix » Sun Jul 13, 2014 12:44 pm I'm not that surprised if she feels comfortable with a selfless provider role no matter how unhealthy it is for her.

[]:             Quote Post by ObsessiveMathsFreak » Thu Jul 17, 2014 6:52 pm A central assumption of this thread is that Rei was in fact in control of the proceedings during Third Impact. But if we view 3I as an allegory for Shinji's suicide attempt, and note the elevated status --not of Rei/Lilith -- but of Eva-01, then it's more likely that it is Shinji who is consciously or not in control of 3I and Rei is simply his very personal transition guide.

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Fri Jul 18, 2014 2:17 am ObsessiveMathsFreak wrote: A central assumption of this thread is that Rei was in fact in control of the proceedings during Third Impact. But if we view 3I as an allegory for Shinji's suicide attempt, and note the elevated status --not of Rei/Lilith -- but of Eva-01, then it's more likely that it is Shinji who is consciously or not in control of 3I and Rei is simply his very personal transition guide.

[]:             Quote Post by ObsessiveMathsFreak » Fri Jul 18, 2014 7:51 am The biggest piece of evidence comes from Fuyutsuki. Episode #26' wrote: FUYUTSUKI: Will it become the Ark to save humankind from the nothingness of Third Impact? Or the demon that destroys us all? Our future lies in the hands of Ikari's son.

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Fri Jul 18, 2014 8:05 am I feel you're equating choice with necessity here. It's clear that Seeds can act on their own (their basic function, confirmed by Adam's actions during 2I), and it's likewise clear that Shinji has no idea as to what's really going on. Rei asks him what he wants, and it happens. He doesn't do any of it on his own. I don't feel this view cheapens Shinji's choice in the least, but either way your reading cheapens Rei as a character since it robs her of any and all agency in the movie. Even her decision to betray Gendo means nothing, since she would be drawn to Shinji whether she wanted it or not. In my view Fuyu's statement refers to the fact that Rei chose Shinji, and will do as he wishes. I can't see any other way to square what we see with the fact that he doesn't actually do anything once he gets in the Eva.

[]:             Quote Post by Sorrow » Fri Jul 18, 2014 10:13 am I concur, the power is in Shinji's hands, but he's not the one with the powers. If you own a genie, then that power is yours so to speak; but you don't actually have any powers. Rei is willing to do what Shinji wants, it's his wish and it's his choice, but she makes it so. That's why there is even a conversation; so she can determine the final outcome that will make Shinji the happiest via him telling her as such. Rei owns the power, Shinji "owns" Rei - in a sense. Though she herself has given him that position. She wants to see the boy happy above all others.

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Fri Jul 18, 2014 1:48 pm I think laying it all on Shinji and treating Rei like a mindless tool really does her a disservice. She's giving him his wish because she chooses to do so, in the manner she chooses to do so, because she values him and wants him to be happy. She thinks he might be making a mistake and thus second guesses him a bit, but ultimately does what he asks. And in the end, I think we finally see what Rei wants, for him to ascend with her, right before he rejects her to return to reality. Rei deserves a share of the blame here. She's earned it damn it, so don't take it from her.

[]:             Quote Post by Sorrow » Fri Jul 18, 2014 1:57 pm It seems quite a sad thought that she may be hoping Shinji wishes to share in her existence but even when he rejects it she obliges his wishes. What's the general opinion on her and Kaworu being able to return from the LCL? They have human bodies and their own souls, not to mention both were involved; is it not possible that they could see themselves in their own hearts as their lilin selves?

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Fri Jul 18, 2014 6:45 pm NemZ wrote: I think laying it all on Shinji and treating Rei like a mindless tool really does her a disservice. She's giving him his wish because she chooses to do so, in the manner she chooses to do so, because she values him and wants him to be happy. She thinks he might be making a mistake and thus second guesses him a bit, but ultimately does what he asks. And in the end, I think we finally see what Rei wants, for him to ascend with her, right before he rejects her to return to reality.

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Fri Jul 18, 2014 7:11 pm Bagheera wrote: I really don't get this. Shinji's options were "stay in Instrumentality and cease to exist as an individual" or "reject Instrumentality and live as a human being." There was no "transcend time and space option with Rei" alternative either presented or rejected.

Rei didn't really explains it to him, in fact she lets his desperation grow until his Ego-barrier is destroyed, which is what truly leads to the instrumentality -not his statements about letting everyb

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Fri Jul 18, 2014 8:05 am I feel you're equating choice with necessity here. It's clear that Seeds can act on their own (their basic function, confirmed by Adam's actions during 2I), and it's likewise clear that Shinji has no idea as to what's really going on. Rei asks him what he wants, and it happens. He doesn't do any of it on his own. I don't feel this view cheapens Shinji's choice in the least, but either way your reading cheapens Rei as a character since it robs her of any and all agency in the movie. Even her decision to betray Gendo means nothing, since she would be drawn to Shinji whether she wanted it or not. In my view Fuyu's statement refers to the fact that Rei chose Shinji, and will do as he wishes. I can't see any other way to square what we see with the fact that he doesn't actually do anything once he gets in the Eva.

[]:             Quote Post by Sorrow » Fri Jul 18, 2014 10:13 am I concur, the power is in Shinji's hands, but he's not the one with the powers. If you own a genie, then that power is yours so to speak; but you don't actually have any powers. Rei is willing to do what Shinji wants, it's his wish and it's his choice, but she makes it so. That's why there is even a conversation; so she can determine the final outcome that will make Shinji the happiest via him telling her as such. Rei owns the power, Shinji "owns" Rei - in a sense. Though she herself has given him that position. She wants to see the boy happy above all others.

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Fri Jul 18, 2014 1:48 pm I think laying it all on Shinji and treating Rei like a mindless tool really does her a disservice. She's giving him his wish because she chooses to do so, in the manner she chooses to do so, because she values him and wants him to be happy. She thinks he might be making a mistake and thus second guesses him a bit, but ultimately does what he asks. And in the end, I think we finally see what Rei wants, for him to ascend with her, right before he rejects her to return to reality. Rei deserves a share of the blame here. She's earned it damn it, so don't take it from her.

[]:             Quote Post by Sorrow » Fri Jul 18, 2014 1:57 pm It seems quite a sad thought that she may be hoping Shinji wishes to share in her existence but even when he rejects it she obliges his wishes. What's the general opinion on her and Kaworu being able to return from the LCL? They have human bodies and their own souls, not to mention both were involved; is it not possible that they could see themselves in their own hearts as their lilin selves?
